stem: By the p-test, \( \int_{[[b]]}^{\infty} \frac{[[c]]}{x^{[[p]]}}\,dx \) converges because \( [[p]] > 1 \). Evaluate it.
distractor_rationales: This forgets the \( [[b]]^{[[p]]-1} \) factor from the lower limit.
This uses \( [[p]] \) instead of \( [[p]]-1 \) in the denominator.
This doubles the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This halves the correct value.

stem: By the p-test, \( \int_{0}^{[[a]]} \frac{[[c]]}{x^{1/[[k]]}}\,dx \) converges even though the integrand blows up at \( x=0 \), since the exponent \( \tfrac{1}{[[k]]} < 1 \). Evaluate it.
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \tfrac{[[k]]}{[[k]]-1} \) but forgets to raise \( [[t]] \) to the power \( [[k]]-1 \).
This drops the leading factor \( \tfrac{[[k]]}{[[k]]-1} \) entirely.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 3/2.

stem: Since \( \frac{[[c]]}{x(x+[[d]])} \) is comparable to \( \frac{[[c]]}{x^{2}} \) for large \( x \), the integral \( \int_{1}^{\infty} \frac{[[c]]}{x(x+[[d]])}\,dx \) converges by comparison with a p-series. Evaluate it exactly.
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by \( [[d]] \) from the partial-fraction coefficient.
This uses \( \ln([[d]]) \) instead of \( \ln([[d]]+1) \).
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This flips the overall sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The convergent improper integral \( \int_{b}^{\infty} \frac{[[c]]}{x^{[[p]]}}\,dx \) equals \( [[target]] \) for a particular lower limit \( b > 0 \). Solve for \( b \).
solution: By the p-test formula, \( \int_{b}^{\infty}\frac{[[c]]}{x^{[[p]]}}\,dx = \frac{[[c]]}{([[p]]-1)b^{[[p]]-1}} \). Setting this equal to \( [[target]] \) and solving gives \( b = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This squares the correct value instead of solving linearly.
